channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Saudi Arabia Restores Major Oil Pipeline After Temporary Disruption
item.description: Following recent regional tensions linked to the ongoing conflict involving Iran, Israel, and Lebanon, Saudi Arabia announced it has successfully restored one of its key oil pipelines to full operatio...

item.title: St Kilda holds off Port Adelaide in tense Gather Round clash as Geelong dominates West Coast
item.description: St Kilda secured a crucial victory over Port Adelaide in the 2026 Gather Round, closing out the match with composure despite a late surge from the Power. The Saints had largely controlled the game but...

item.title: Batangas dominates Caloocan in WMPBL opener despite Bernardino's absence
item.description: Batangas City, even without their key player Bernardino, delivered a commanding performance in the opening game of their Women’s Maharlika Pilipinas Basketball League (WMPBL) title defense, defeating ...

item.title: Russia and Ukraine Report Ceasefire Violations During Easter Weekend
item.description: During the Orthodox Easter weekend, Russia and Ukraine exchanged accusations over violations of a Kremlin-declared 32-hour ceasefire. Russian President Vladimir Putin announced a halt to hostilities f...

item.title: Man Charged with Murder Following Primrose Hill Stabbing
item.description: A man has been charged with the murder of Finbar Sullivan, who tragically died after being stabbed in Primrose Hill, north London. Metropolitan Police confirmed the arrest of Oliuwadamilola Ogunyankin...

item.title: China announces measures to boost exchanges with Taiwan after KMT leader’s trip
item.description: China has unveiled ten new incentive measures aimed at Taiwan after a visit by Cheng Li-wun, chairwoman of Taiwan’s Kuomintang (KMT), the main opposition party. The incentives include easing tourist r...

item.title: Edward Motale reassures Orlando Pirates supporters after hard-fought draw with Richards Bay
item.description: Former Orlando Pirates captain and club legend Edward Motale has called on the team’s supporters to remain calm after the Buccaneers were held to a 2–2 draw against Richards Bay in their Premiership c...

item.title: U.S. Signals Potential Naval Blockade in Strait of Hormuz Following Failed Iran Negotiations
item.description: Following a weekend of intensive negotiations in Islamabad, the peace talks between the United States and Iran have collapsed, prompting President Donald Trump to announce a potential blockade of the ...

item.title: Court officially declares missing detective as deceased after years of absence
item.description: The High Court has finally declared a detective from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) dead after he had been missing for several years. The officer, who disappeared under unclear circu...

item.title: Engineering Admission in Karnataka Based on JEE and KCET Scores
item.description: This article aims to inform students about the top engineering colleges in Karnataka and the process of securing admission through JEE and KCET scores. Karnataka, being a hub for technical education, ...

item.title: DFA clarifies transparency in Philippine–China Coast Guard discussions
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) has officially denied allegations that negotiations regarding amendments to the 2016 memorandum of understanding (MOU) between the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) ...

item.title: Gilas Women secure spot in 2026 FIBA 3×3 World Cup after defeating Singapore
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – After an eight-year hiatus, Gilas Pilipinas Women have qualified for the FIBA 3×3 World Cup, set to take place in Warsaw, Poland, this June. The team secured their spot with a de...

item.title: Wes Streeting advises focus on Trump's actions amid strained UK-US relations over Iran
item.description: Labour's Wes Streeting has criticised US President Donald Trump's social media activity, describing it as 'incendiary', 'provocative', and 'outrageous', but advised the British public to focus on his ...

item.title: Prime Minister Albanese Visits Brunei and Malaysia to Strengthen Fuel and Resource Security
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is embarking on his second fuel security mission within a week, visiting Brunei and Malaysia following assurances from Singapore that it would not restrict liquid fuel ...

item.title: CF Montreal falls 2-1 to Union in MLS home opener amid slow season start
item.description: CF Montreal suffered a disappointing result in their MLS home opener, falling 2-1 to the Philadelphia Union. The team’s struggle to find form early in the season was evident as they failed to capitali...

item.title: APC narrows list to three candidates for Kano deputy governor position
item.description: Fresh wahala dey for Kano political circle as APC don shortlist three people wey fit become new deputy governor. Dem don submit names of Kabiru Alhassan Rurum, Murtala Sule Garo, and Rabiu Suleiman Bi...

item.title: Australian Budget Focus Needed on Supply-Side Economics Amidst Geopolitical Uncertainty
item.description: With significant financial and geopolitical volatility impacting the global economy, particularly following Donald Trump's actions in Iran, Australian policymakers and businesses are facing heightened...

item.title: Examining the intelligence lapses that allowed the Rwandan genocide to unfold
item.description: The article from Nation Africa focuses on how lapses in intelligence sharing and political inaction contributed to the tragic Rwandan genocide of 1994. It explains that local and international intelli...

item.title: Geordin Hill-Lewis elected as new DA federal leader
item.description: Geordin Hill-Lewis, the 39-year-old mayor of Cape Town, has been elected as the new federal leader of the Democratic Alliance (DA), South Africa’s second-largest political party. His election, announc...

item.title: King Charles urged to deliver a frank message to the United States during his upcoming Congress speech
item.description: Simon Tisdall argues that King Charles III’s forthcoming visit to the United States should be more than mere ceremony. Scheduled to address a joint session of Congress later this month, the king’s app...
